## Trans / Non-binary comfort expressing political views analysis
## ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# Source utilities
source("C:/Users/math_/.claude/skills/survey-search/scripts/survey_utils.R")
source("C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/ai_interns/templates/meo_theme.R")

# ── 1. Load and clean ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

catalogue <- load_catalogue()
data <- read.csv("harmonized_data.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM")
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

data <- clean_survey_data(data, catalogue)
data <- weight_survey_data(data, catalogue, method = "rake")

# DO NOT overwrite harmonized_data.csv — models.R reads it too

# ── 2. Recode variables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Check actual comfort scale values
cat("\npol_speech_1 values:\n")
print(table(data$pol_speech_1, useNA = "ifany"))

# Actual data uses 0-based coding: 0=Not at all, 1=Not very, 2=Somewhat, 3=Comfortable, 4=Very
# Recode to 1-5 scale
recode_comfort_0based <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 0 ~ 1L,  # Not at all comfortable
    x == 1 ~ 2L,  # Not very comfortable
    x == 2 ~ 3L,  # Somewhat comfortable
    x == 3 ~ 4L,  # Comfortable
    x == 4 ~ 5L,  # Very comfortable
    TRUE ~ NA_integer_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    pol_speech_1_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_1),
    pol_speech_2_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_2),
    pol_speech_3_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_3)
  )

# Kirk items (only in 2025_10_Tracking) — already 1-5 scale, no recode needed
if ("chilled_kirk_1" %in% names(data)) {
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(
      chilled_kirk_1_r = as.integer(chilled_kirk_1),
      chilled_kirk_2_r = as.integer(chilled_kirk_2),
      chilled_kirk_3_r = as.integer(chilled_kirk_3)
    )
}

---

## Context

On February 10, 2026, 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar killed her mother and half-brother at their home in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, before walking to Tumbler Ridge Secondary School, where she killed six people and wounded 27 others before dying by suicide. The attack was Canada's deadliest school shooting since the 1989 École Polytechnique massacre.

When RCMP confirmed the following day that Van Rootselaar was a trans woman, a now-documented information manipulation playbook activated within hours. A fraudulent X account was renamed to match the shooter's YouTube channel and seeded with white supremacist content — briefly misleading the Anti-Defamation League. An unrelated trans woman in Ontario who shared the shooter's surname was misidentified across X, Reddit, and briefly by Radio-Canada. US-based influencers — including Libs of TikTok and Elon Musk — amplified viral charts claiming trans people are disproportionately responsible for mass shootings, a claim contradicted by the available data: trans-identified individuals account for fewer than 0.1% of mass shooters (5 of 5,748 since 2012). Rebel News characterized the attack as "a predictable act of trans madness."

The Institute for Strategic Dialogue (ISD) documented a spike in anti-LGBTQ+ posts from a baseline of roughly 20 per day to 233 on February 11 — exceeding 10,000 posts within 24 hours. This trajectory follows a six-phase information manipulation sequence observed after prior incidents involving trans-identified shooters (Nashville, 2023; Annunciation, 2025) and the Charlie Kirk assassination (2025): pre-identification speculation, misidentification of an uninvolved individual, fake account creation, statistical manipulation, policy weaponization, and the framing of fact-checks as institutional cover-ups.

This report examines the downstream effects of this incident on the political expression of trans and non-binary Canadians using longitudinal survey data.

---

- **Neutral, clinical tone** throughout — consistent with an academic report analyzing survey data.

## Context

On February 10, 2026, 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar killed her mother and half-brother at their home in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, then walked to Tumbler Ridge Secondary School, where she killed six people and wounded 27 others before dying by suicide. It was Canada's deadliest school shooting since the 1989 École Polytechnique massacre.

The next day, RCMP confirmed that Van Rootselaar was a trans woman. Within hours, a familiar information manipulation playbook kicked in. A fraudulent X account was renamed to match the shooter's YouTube channel and filled with white supremacist content — briefly fooling the Anti-Defamation League. An unrelated trans woman in Ontario who shared the shooter's surname was misidentified across X, Reddit, and briefly by Radio-Canada. US influencers — including Libs of TikTok and Elon Musk — pushed viral charts claiming trans people are disproportionately responsible for mass shootings. The numbers tell a different story: trans people account for fewer than 0.1% of mass shooters (5 of 5,748 since 2012). Rebel News called it "a predictable act of trans madness."

The Institute for Strategic Dialogue (ISD) tracked anti-LGBTQ+ posts jumping from around 20 per day to 233 on February 11, topping 10,000 posts within 24 hours. The pattern matches a six-phase playbook seen after previous incidents — Nashville (2023), Annunciation (2025), and the Charlie Kirk assassination (2025): first, speculation about the shooter's identity; then misidentification of an innocent person; fake account creation; statistical manipulation; policy weaponization; and finally, framing fact-checks as cover-ups.

This report looks at how that wave of backlash affected the willingness of trans and non-binary Canadians to express their political views, drawing on longitudinal survey data.

---

## The Incident

A fake X account changed its name to match a YouTube channel belonging to the Tumbler Ridge shooter and featured numerous racist, white supremacist and antisemitic posts. The shooting left eight people dead and two more seriously injured.

## The ADL's Initial Response and Retraction

The Anti-Defamation League (ADL) published a report Wednesday that initially referenced the X profile and said the shooter had an interest in "gore, guns, and white supremacy". However, some posts from that account appeared to have a different username (@HisuiPapist) suggesting that whoever owned it had changed the name to match the YouTube account in an apparent effort to impersonate the shooter.

The ADL issued a correction Thursday afternoon after being contacted by CBC, removing "white supremacy" from the headline and writing that "Upon further investigation, that X account has been found not credible".

## Expert Skepticism

Experts at the Institute for Strategic Dialogue (ISD) are not confident the account actually belonged to the shooter, with a senior research manager stating "I don't buy it at this stage, based on the social media analysis I've done". The rhetoric on the X account doesn't match up with the content on other social media accounts the analysts are more confident belonged to the shooter, as the X account was deeply integrated into white supremacist ideologies which did not match up with what they found on other social media accounts, particularly Reddit.

In February 2026, a shooting occurred at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School in British Columbia, and police identified the shooter as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, a resident of the quiet British Columbia community of Tumbler Ridge.

Based on the search results, here's what fact-checkers and experts have found about the statistic you're asking about:

## The Basic Statistic

According to the Gun Violence Archive (GVA), there were 5,748 mass shootings between Jan 1, 2013 and September 15, 2025 in the U.S. Of that number, there have been five confirmed transgender shooters, which means that transgender individuals were responsible for less than 0.1% of incidents over that time period.

## How Experts Debunk the Misrepresentation

The "5 of 5748" statistic itself is accurate according to professional mass shooting databases, but criminology professors and data analysts have criticized charts presenting this data, noting that the sample size is too small, the definitions are flawed, and the per-capita rates are misleading.

According to a 2024 analysis by the Poynter Institute, transgender people represent 0.1–1.5% of shooters in the US, and make up 0.5–1.6% of the population. This means transgender people are actually **underrepresented** as perpetrators when compared to their share of the population.

## The Key Finding

Experts on gun violence agree that transgender people do not represent a disproportionate threat, and there is no evidence to suggest they are more likely to commit violent crimes than the general population.

## Key Findings from the Institute for Strategic Dialogue (ISD)

Canadian users made more than 10,000 anti-LGBTQ+ posts the day after the Tumbler Ridge attack, which was the most pronounced spike in anti-LGBTQ+ hate speech between August 2025 and February 2026. Globally, more than 154,000 posts were recorded after the attack.

After the 10 February mass casualty attack in Tumbler Ridge, the perpetrator was quickly identified as a transgender woman. Almost immediately, there was a wave of activity on major social media which sought to portray the event as an example of a broader threat posed by the transgender community.

## Response from Canadian Domestic Extremists

The ISD analysis specifically tracked Canadian domestic extremist accounts and found alarming patterns: In the week leading up to the Tumbler Ridge attack, Canadian domestic extremists made an average of 20 anti-LGBTQ+ posts per day. On 11 February, when news of the attack and the perpetrator's identity became widely known, that increased more than 1,000 percent to 233 posts.

## Broader Context

The Tumbler Ridge shooting coincided with the third-largest spike in anti-LGBTQ+ hate speech between 15 August 2025 and 15 February 2026. The largest spike followed the mass shooting at the Annunciation Church in Minneapolis (US) in late August–when it was discovered that the perpetrator of that attack was transgender.

Based on the search results, here's what we know about the 2026 Tumbler Ridge shooting in Canada:

## Overview

On February 10, 2026, a mass shooting occurred in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Canada, in which Jesse Van Rootselaar killed her mother and half-brother at their home before going to Tumbler Ridge Secondary School, where she killed six people and injured twenty-seven others before killing herself.

## Significance

The incident was the deadliest mass shooting in Canada since the Nova Scotia attacks in 2020 and the deadliest school shooting in Canada since the École Polytechnique massacre in 1989. More specifically, the shooting at the small, rural high school is the second deadliest school shooting in Canadian history, behind the massacre that left 14 dead at L'Ecole Polytechnique in Montreal on Dec. 6, 1989.

## Details About the Victims

Six victims were found dead inside the high school. Five of the victims were children: Abel Mwansa, 12; Ezekiel Schofield, 13; Kylie Smith, 12; Zoey Benoit, 12; and Ticaria Lampert, 12. There was one educator killed in the school, identified as Shannda Aviugana-Durand, 39. The shooter's mother and half-brother were killed at the family's home, identified as Jennifer Strang, 39, and Emmett Jacobs, 11.

## About the Shooter

Police identified the suspected shooter as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, who was found dead at the school from what police described as a self‑inflicted injury. Van Rootselaar previously attended the school but dropped out about four years ago, according to Royal Canadian Mounted Police Deputy Commissioner Dwayne McDonald.

I found search results related to anti-trans misinformation about mass shootings and violent incidents. Here's what the search revealed:

## Key Patterns in Anti-Trans Misinformation

Right-wing internet users, media outlets, and politicians regularly spread false claims that mass shooting perpetrators are transgender, often starting before the release of any substantive information from authorities.

### Misidentification Tactic

The cycle usually starts with an anonymous or burner account, often with a patriotic username, no verifiable identity, and a history of inflammatory posts, which upload videos or images appearing to feature a transgender person behaving in a shocking or inappropriate way. Within minutes of a mass shooting, random people on social media, particularly Twitter (now X), would falsely claim that the shooter was trans, and put up pictures of a trans person pulled from Reddit randomly, a pattern that began with the 2022 Uvalde school shooting.

### Use of Fake Content

Content used includes AI-generated faces, clips of cisgender people inaccurately labeled as trans, unrelated incidents with fake captions implying transgender identity, and footage spliced or edited to create a false narrative.

### Policy Weaponization

Members of the second Trump administration have suggested policies on the basis of these claims, including for the Department of Justice to revoke trans people's right to possess firearms.

### Real-World Impacts

According to subject-matter experts, disinformation casting transgender people as violent is used to legitimize anti-trans violence and legislation, particularly as part of a broader anti-LGBTQ backlash in the United States.

content.content:
# Recurring Patterns in Trans Misinformation

The article does **not** describe a formally named "six-phase playbook" or similar structured framework. However, it does document recurring tactics that follow a loose sequence after violent events:

1. **Rapid misidentification**: "Claims are often spread on social media in the immediate aftermath of events before full facts are available."

2. **False attribution**: Multiple examples show perpetrators falsely blamed as transgender (Uvalde shooting, Lakewood Church shooting, Potomac River collision).

3. **Statistical manipulation**: Charts with "fabricated" data went viral claiming to show increased trans violence rates. The Gun Violence Archive founder stated the numbers were "painfully inaccurate."

4. **Policy weaponization**: Following these narratives, officials proposed banning trans people from firearm ownership and classifying "transgender ideology" as domestic terrorism.

5. **Amplification infrastructure**: Right-wing figures (Trump Jr., Raichik, Heritage Foundation) and platforms like X distribute these claims broadly.

The article emphasizes this represents a "years-long effort" to characterize trans people as "exceptionally violent" but treats these as coordinated tactics rather than a formally documented sequential model.

content.text:
Here's what I've verified and what I couldn't pin down:

**Confirmed with sources:**

| Claim | Source |
|-------|--------|
| Basic facts (date, victims, suicide) | [CBC](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/livestory/active-shooter-alert-tumbler-ridge-secondary-school-bc-live-updates-9.7083740), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Tumbler_Ridge_shooting) |
| Deadliest school shooting since Polytechnique | [CBC](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/school-shooting-ten-dead-british-columbia-9.7084222), [Time](https://time.com/7377687/canada-shooting-tumbler-ridge/) |
| RCMP confirmed trans woman (Deputy Commissioner McDonald) | [RCMP update](https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350292), [CTV](https://www.ctvnews.ca/canada/article/heres-what-we-know-about-the-tumbler-ridge-shooter/) |
| Fake X account / ADL fooled then retracted | [CBC Investigates](https://www.cbc.ca/news/investigates/tumbler-ridge-shooter-x-hoax-9.7087930), [The Forward](https://forward.com/fast-forward/805024/adl-retracts-tumbler-ridge-shooting-antisemitism-claim/) |
| Misidentified Ontario woman (Zylii Strang) | [Yahoo/CP](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/ontario-resident-misidentified-tumbler-ridge-180335586.html), [Global News](https://globalnews.ca/news/11669400/misinformation-tumbler-ridge-shooting/) |
| Libs of TikTok speculated before ID; Musk shared chart | [Newsweek](https://www.newsweek.com/canadian-police-face-anger-response-trans-school-shooters-identity-11509338), [LGBTQ Nation](https://www.lgbtqnation.com/2026/02/the-tumbler-ridge-tragedy-has-fueled-vile-anti-trans-hate-but-trans-shooters-are-extremely-rare/) |
| Trans people < 0.1% of mass shooters (5/5,748) | [GLAAD fact sheet](https://glaad.org/debunking-trans-terrorism/), [Canadian Press fact check](https://www.thecanadianpressnews.ca/fact_checking/fact-file-no-evidence-of-trans-shooter-epidemic/article_7e77efbc-6cf8-525c-bb12-c5aa54a30a5e.html) |
| "Predictable act of trans madness" — Lise Merle on Rebel News livestream | [The Walrus](https://thewalrus.ca/tumbler-ridge-is-being-used-to-spread-anti-trans-misinformation/) (Walrus blocked fetch, but search snippets confirm; also [Rebel News livestream page](https://www.rebelnews.com/buffalo_roundtable_february_11_2026)) |
| ISD: ~20 posts/day baseline → 233 on Feb 11, 10,000+ in 24h | [ISD Digital Dispatch](https://www.isdglobal.org/digital-dispatch/quantitative-analysis-anti-lgbtq-hate-tumbler-ridge-shooting/), [National Observer](https://www.nationalobserver.com/2026/03/13/news/canadas-far-right-fuelled-surge-hate-after-tumbler-ridge-mass-shooting-researchers) |

**Not confirmed:**

| Claim | Issue |
|-------|-------|
| "Six-phase playbook" as a named framework | I couldn't find this as a formal, published framework. Wikipedia describes these as **recurring tactics** (misidentification, fake accounts, statistical manipulation, policy weaponization, etc.) but not as a codified "six-phase" model. |

**One correction:** The quote is attributed to **Lise Merle** (a Rebel News commentator on a livestream), not Rebel News editorially. Worth being precise about that.

Want me to revise the Context draft with these references inline, or do you want to handle the referencing yourself now that you have the URLs?

## Context

On February 10, 2026, 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar killed her mother and half-brother at their home in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, then walked to Tumbler Ridge Secondary School, where she killed six people and wounded 27 others before dying by suicide ([CBC](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/livestory/active-shooter-alert-tumbler-ridge-secondary-school-bc-live-updates-9.7083740)). It was Canada's deadliest school shooting since the 1989 École Polytechnique massacre ([CBC](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/school-shooting-ten-dead-british-columbia-9.7084222)).

The next day, RCMP Deputy Commissioner Dwayne McDonald confirmed that Van Rootselaar was a trans woman ([RCMP](https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350292)). Within hours, a familiar information manipulation pattern kicked in. A fraudulent X account was renamed to match the shooter's YouTube channel and filled with white supremacist content — briefly fooling the Anti-Defamation League, which published a report citing the account before retracting it ([CBC Investigates](https://www.cbc.ca/news/investigates/tumbler-ridge-shooter-x-hoax-9.7087930); [The Forward](https://forward.com/fast-forward/805024/adl-retracts-tumbler-ridge-shooting-antisemitism-claim/)). An unrelated trans woman in Ontario who shared the shooter's surname was misidentified across X, Reddit, and briefly by Radio-Canada ([Yahoo/CP](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/ontario-resident-misidentified-tumbler-ridge-180335586.html); [Global News](https://globalnews.ca/news/11669400/misinformation-tumbler-ridge-shooting/)). US influencers — including Libs of TikTok and Elon Musk — pushed viral charts claiming trans people are disproportionately responsible for mass shootings ([Newsweek](https://www.newsweek.com/canadian-police-face-anger-response-trans-school-shooters-identity-11509338)). The numbers tell a different story: trans people account for fewer than 0.1% of mass shooters — 5 of 5,748 since 2012 ([GLAAD](https://glaad.org/debunking-trans-terrorism/); [Canadian Press](https://www.thecanadianpressnews.ca/fact_checking/fact-file-no-evidence-of-trans-shooter-epidemic/article_7e77efbc-6cf8-525c-bb12-c5aa54a30a5e.html)). On a Rebel News livestream, commentator Lise Merle called it "a predictable act of trans madness" ([The Walrus](https://thewalrus.ca/tumbler-ridge-is-being-used-to-spread-anti-trans-misinformation/)).

The Institute for Strategic Dialogue tracked anti-LGBTQ+ posts from Canadian domestic extremist accounts jumping from around 20 per day to 233 on February 11, topping 10,000 posts within 24 hours ([ISD](https://www.isdglobal.org/digital-dispatch/quantitative-analysis-anti-lgbtq-hate-tumbler-ridge-shooting/)). The pattern — pre-identification speculation, misidentification of an innocent person, fake account creation, statistical manipulation, policy weaponization, and framing fact-checks as cover-ups — has recurred after previous incidents involving trans-identified shooters, including Nashville (2023) and the Annunciation church shooting (2025) ([Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Misinformation_about_violence_by_transgender_people)).

This report looks at how that wave of backlash affected the willingness of trans and non-binary Canadians to express their political views, drawing on longitudinal survey data.

---

Two changes from your original framing:

- **"Six-phase playbook"** is now described as a recurring pattern rather than a named framework, since I couldn't verify it as a published model.
- **Rebel News quote** is attributed specifically to Lise Merle on a livestream, not to Rebel News editorially.
